title: Session-scoped injection dedup with fail-safe degradation
context: Pre-edit hook re-fires on every edit, repeatedly injecting identical 10-16KB grounding blocks 20+ times per session. This repetition buries the agent's working context under duplicate data, competing with productive work for token budget.
decision: Cache injection content hashes per Claude-Code session in tmpdir. On first injection or content change, emit full grounding ('full'). On identical repeat, return 'delta' so caller emits a one-liner instead. Any cache error (corrupt file, missing session ID, unwritable tmpdir) defaults to 'full' — grounding always wins over dedup. Kill switch: HUNCH_HOOK_DEDUP=0.
consequences: Agents see full grounding once, then compact deltas on repeats within a session
Context budget freed for productive work instead of repetitive injection
Deny decisions bypass cache entirely; always re-checked fresh
Session ID must be threaded through hook events
Requires tmpdir write access; read failures degrade safely to full grounding
alternatives_rejected: Disable hook on repeats: loses grounding if records change mid-session
Global cache across sessions: wastes space, risks stale data
Strict cache validation: hook failures would break edits instead of defaulting safe
Always emit delta: no way to recover if underlying records change

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Disable hook on repeats: loses grounding if records change mid-session
Global cache across sessions: wastes space, risks stale data
Strict cache validation: hook failures would break edits instead of defaulting safe
Always emit delta: no way to recover if underlying records change
